Requirement: WHILE VehSpd is within [0 kph, 250 kph], THE SYSTEM SHALL process the VehSpd value within [50 ms].
Rationale: The requirement lacks specificity in defining what 'process the VehSpd value within [50 ms]' entails. It does not clarify whether the 50 ms refers to latency, throughput, or another metric, making verification ambiguous and non-testable per INCOSE guidelines.

Requirement: IF VehSpd > 250 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_HI_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_HI - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) which violates EARS syntax rule requiring a single system response per requirement. INCOSE guidelines also emphasize atomicity of requirements for clarity and testability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd < 0 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_LO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_LO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) under a single condition, violating EARS syntax which requires one atomic system response per requirement. INCOSE guidelines also emphasize atomicity for testability and traceability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd AliveCounter is unchanged for [3 consecutive cycles], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_ALC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_ALC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement combines multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) into a single statement, violating the INCOSE guideline that requirements should be atomic (address one specific aspect). Each action should be a separate requirement for clarity and verifiability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd CRC is invalid, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_CRC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_CRC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) combined into a single statement. INCOSE guidelines require requirements to be atomic (single functional aspect per requirement) to ensure clarity, testability, and traceability. This violates the principle of having one requirement address one specific system behavior.

Requirement: When VehSpd < 10 kph, THE LKA shall transition to active state
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: Requirement contains valid structural components: Trigger (VehSpd < 10 kph), System (LKA), Modality (shall), and System Response (transition to active state). Complies with INCOSE guidelines for clarity, testability, and atomicity. EARS syntax rules are satisfied with proper precondition-system-response structure.
